concept_records.canonical_key: anomaly driven principle augmentation anomaly driven principle augmentation if an experimental observation contradicts the current set of scientific principles the system must expand the principle space to incorporate a new rule that explains the anomaly rather than discarding the observation as w f

payload.abstract_signature: If an experimental observation contradicts the current set of scientific principles, the system must expand the principle space to incorporate a new rule that explains the anomaly rather than discarding the observation as error.
payload.argument: If an experimental observation contradicts the current set of scientific principles, the system must expand the principle space to incorporate a new rule that explains the anomaly rather than discarding the observation as error.
payload.canonical_key: if experimental observation contradicts current set of scientific principles system must expand principle space to incorporate new rule explains anomaly rather than discarding obse
payload.discussion: The principle addresses the rigidity of fixed-prior systems by institutionalizing the response to failure. It posits that anomalies are not failures of the experiment but failures of the current theory, necessitating an update to the theory itself. This creates a feedback loop where the system's knowledge base grows precisely at the points of its previous ignorance, ensuring continuous adaptation to complex scientific domains.
payload.evidence: The text notes that existing approaches 'discard valid observations as errors' when they contradict priors, whereas the proposed method uses an 'anomaly-driven augmentation mechanism' to 'refine their theoretical worldview' and handle 'unexpected results to expand its conceptual boundaries'.
payload.name: Anomaly-Driven Principle Augmentation

concept_records.canonical_key: information directed hypothesis selection information directed hypothesis selection hypothesis generation is guided by gaussian process based uncertainty minimization to select experiments that maximally reduce ambiguity in the principle space this ensures that every experimental step provides high

payload.canonical_key: hypothesis generation is guided by gaussian process based uncertainty minimization to select experiments maximally reduce ambiguity in principle space ensures every experimental st
payload.core_idea: Hypothesis generation is guided by Gaussian Process-based uncertainty minimization to select experiments that maximally reduce ambiguity in the principle space. This ensures that every experimental step provides high information gain regarding the validity of evolving principles.
payload.discussion: By focusing on uncertainty minimization, the agent achieves an 83.3% speedup in convergence steps compared to static methods. The efficiency gain stems from the ability to identify the most informative experiments early in the process, thereby pruning the search tree of invalid principles rapidly. This strategy effectively manages the 'inefficiency in hypothesizing' caused by repetitive application of inadequate prior knowledge.
payload.evidence: The abstract explicitly mentions 'integrating Information-Directed Hypothesis Selection via Gaussian Process' as a core component that enables the system to attain an '83.3% speedup in convergence step via significantly reduced sample complexity'.
payload.idea_text: Hypothesis generation is guided by Gaussian Process-based uncertainty minimization to select experiments that maximally reduce ambiguity in the principle space. This ensures that every experimental step provides high information gain regarding the validity of evolving principles.
payload.mechanism: The selection mechanism models the relationship between principles and observed outcomes using a Gaussian Process. It calculates an acquisition function that prioritizes hypothesis candidates expected to yield the highest reduction in posterior uncertainty. This prevents the agent from wasting resources on experiments that merely confirm existing beliefs or explore irrelevant areas of the hypothesis space.
payload.source_paper_title: Principle-Evolvable Scientific Discovery via Uncertainty Minimization
payload.source_work_title: Principle-Evolvable Scientific Discovery via Uncertainty Minimization

upload_decisions.title: Principle-Evolvable Scientific Discovery via Uncertainty Minimization

identity.canonical_title: Principle-Evolvable Scientific Discovery via Uncertainty Minimization
identity.title_norm: principle evolvable scientific discovery via uncertainty minimization

work_version_records.abstract: Large Language Model (LLM)-based scientific agents have accelerated scientific discovery, yet they often suffer from significant inefficiencies due to adherence to fixed initial priors. Existing approaches predominantly operate within a static hypothesis space, which restricts the discovery of novel phenomena, resulting in computational waste when baseline theories fail. To address this, we propose shifting the focus from searching hypotheses to evolving the underlying scientific principles. We present PiEvo, a principle-evolvable framework that treats scientific discovery as Bayesian optimization over an expanding principle space. By integrating Information-Directed Hypothesis Selection via Gaussian Process and an anomaly-driven augmentation mechanism, PiEvo enables agents to autonomously refine their theoretical worldview. Evaluation across four benchmarks demonstrates that PiEvo (1) achieves an average solution quality of up to 90.81%~93.15%, representing a 29.7%~31.1% improvement over the state-of-the-art, (2) attains an 83.3% speedup in convergence step via significantly reduced sample complexity by optimizing the compact principle space, and (3) maintains robust performance across diverse scientific domains and LLM backbones. Code is publicly available at \hyperlink{https://github.com/amair-lab/PiEvo}{github.com/amair-lab/PiEvo}.

By integrating Information-Directed Hypothesis Selection via Gaussian Process and an anomaly-driven augmentation mechanism, PiEvo enables agents to autonomously refine their theoretical worldview. Evaluation across four benchmarks demonstrates that PiEvo (1) achieves an average solution quality of up to 90.81%~93.15%, representing a 29.7%~31.1% improvement over the state-of-the-art, (2) attains an 83.3% speedup in convergence step via significantly reduced sample complexity by optimizing the compact principle space, and (3) maintains robust performance across diverse scientific domains and LLM backbones.
